sources.label: Yahoo Finance UK - Rotork maintains 2026 outlook after steady Q1 (data centre and water demand cited)
Yahoo Finance - Rotork analyst rebalancing (BNP upgrade vs Barclays caution)
Rotork investor relations - RNS announcements (no material releases since June 6)

verdicts.rankRationale: No RNS or material news since June 6; today's -1.49% is drift, not a fundamental event. The strict latent-node case (actuators/flow control feeding coolant and water loops) is unchanged from yesterday's hold, and the stock remains un-rerated at -6.8% YTD, so residual alpha is intact but not improved by new evidence.
verdicts.bull: Q1 update confirmed data centre and water infrastructure projects are already supporting Chemical, Process & Industrial and Water & Power demand, validating the latent AI-infrastructure pathway before the market prices it. A negative YTD on an unchanged thesis keeps the entry asymmetric.
verdicts.neutral: Guidance for 2026 was merely reiterated and order intake edged lower on EMEA oil & gas softness; the AI/data-centre exposure is real but still a minority of mix, so the name drifts with UK industrials until a disclosed data-centre or liquid-cooling order quantifies it.
verdicts.bear: Oil & gas remains the dominant end market and its order softness could swamp data-centre growth; if the AI cooling pathway never becomes a disclosed revenue line, this stays a fairly valued flow-control industrial with no latent-node optionality, and Barclays-style caution on execution gains traction.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Half-year results / next trading update quantifying data-centre and water infrastructure order intake, or a disclosed cooling-loop actuation win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued oil & gas order weakness in EMEA dragging group orders lower and overshadowing the nascent data-centre exposure.
